creator: keramida@ceid.upatras.gr
text:
On 2008-02-12 10:49, Matthew Luckie <mjl@luckie.org.nz> wrote:
>Giorgos Keramidas wrote:
>>On 2008-02-11 17:41, Matthew Luckie <mjl@luckie.org.nz> wrote:
>>> On Sun Feb 10 19:52:53 UTC 2008 keramida wrote:
>>>> Grab this PR. I will make sure the patch is sent upstream too,
>>>> and get the patch tested+committed on FreeBSD.
>>> I sent patch upstream.  It has been accepted and committed.
>>>
>>> http://cvs.savannah.gnu.org/viewvc/emacs/configure.in?root=emacs&r1=1.444.2.8&r2=1.444.2.9
>>
>> Nice, thanks.
>>
>> Then we can either commit this one, or update to a more recent
>> snapshot of CVS Emacs, and it will be included in the original
>> unpatched source :-)
>
> I'd like to at least see the patch included in the PR go into
> ports/editors/emacs as a patch to that version.  That's the version I
> personally use.

That sounds sensible enough.

I was talking about editors/emacs-devel for the latest cvs changes of Emacs
(i.e. to get a new version now that the unicode-2 branch is merged into
upstream).  But that's an `experimental' version of Emacs, so I have no
problem with including the patch to the main `editors/emacs' port.

Thanks for the clarification.  I was confused :)
